key_insight: The paper learns how uncertainty changes with both context and decisions using nonparametric models, then plugs those predictions into a residual-corrected stochastic optimization framework.
engineering_relevance: It enables more reliable decision-making in systems where demand, costs, or constraints depend on actions and external context, such as pricing, inventory, and facility location.
method: Use kNN, CART, or ReLU networks to estimate decision- and context-dependent uncertainty.
Add empirical residuals to point predictions in an ER-DD-SAA formulation to hedge prediction error.
Derive exact mixed-integer programming reformulations; for kNN two-stage problems, use a Benders + constraint-generation solver (BD-CG).
evidence_signal: Experiments on a newsvendor-with-pricing problem and a two-stage facility location problem show better out-of-sample performance than a parametric benchmark and improved computational tractability.
watch_item: The approach relies on accurate nonparametric learning and MIP reformulations, so performance and scalability may degrade with very high-dimensional data or weak training coverage.
paper_title: Contextual Stochastic Optimization with Decision-Dependent Uncertainty via Nonparametric Learning
